case_name: Calderdale & Huddersfield NHS Foundation Trust vMetcalf [2021] EWHC 611 (QB) (11 February 2021)

evidence_index.text: Ms Metcalf pursued a clinical negligence claim against the Trust, as a result of a delay in diagnosing her cauda equina syndrome in 2012.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: She attended the Accident and Emergency department of the Hospital on 30 June, 2 July and 4 July 2012 but she was not correctly diagnosed until she went again on 5 July and was given an MRI scan.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The Trust admitted liability at the pre-action stage, based on a failure of care on 4 July only.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: An early interim payment of £75,000 was agreed.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The Trust amended its Defence to allege that Ms Metcalf's claims fell to be dismissed because they were rooted in fundamental dishonesty.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Her claims should be dismissed because of fundamental dishonesty and to repay the interim payment of £75,000.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Appropriate punishment for faking evidence in support a claim inflated by some £5 million can only be achieved by immediate custody.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's analysis

evidence_index.text: I sentence you to 6 months imprisonment. You must serve 3 months immediately and you will then be entitled to release for the remainder of the term.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

initial_state.chief_complaint: Severe lower back pain and neurological symptoms
initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Patient reports worsening lower back pain with associated neurological symptoms over several days.

focused_exam.neurological: Examination mentioned but findings not specified

critical_findings: Neurological symptoms suggestive of nerve compression

requestables.name: MRI Spine
requestables.description: MRI scan to assess for cauda equina syndrome

reveal.result_summary: Imaging performed but findings not detailed in judgment
reveal.clinical_significance: Necessary to confirm diagnosis of cauda equina syndrome

timeline_phases.name: Emergency Presentation
timeline_phases.description: Patient presents to ED with acute symptoms

key_events.event: Patient attended ED on 30 June, 2 July, and 4 July 2012

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient in ED with severe lower back pain and neurological symptoms
decision_points.prompt: What imaging investigation should be ordered?

options.clinical_reasoning: MRI is the standard investigation for suspected cauda equina syndrome
Delay in imaging can worsen outcomes in cauda equina syndrome

expected_action_court.description: MRI spine should have been ordered immediately
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Prompt imaging is critical in suspected cauda equina syndrome

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Delay in imaging led to worsened outcomes
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Immediate MRI should have been performed
explanation.legal_standard_applied: Breach of duty due to delay in diagnosis

patient_outcome.description: Patient developed worsened neurological symptoms due to delay

key_findings: Breach of duty in failing to diagnose promptly
Causation established - delay led to worsened condition

malpractice_determination.point_of_failure: Failure to order MRI imaging promptly
malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Immediate MRI would have led to earlier intervention

court_findings.legal_significance: Delay in diagnosis led to worsened outcomes
